title: Solving equations graphically: graphing calculator
description: Solve equations of the form f(x)=g(x) by graphing both f and g.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$2^x=2+3x$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 3.7$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=2^x$ and $g(x)=2+3x$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/p42amgubsl) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -0.4$ and where $x\approx 3.7$.

We already know $x\approx 3.7$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx -0.4$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx -0.4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$\sqrt[3]{x}=|x-2|-1$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 0.3$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=\sqrt[3]{x}$ and $g(x)=|x-2|-1$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/qiotlsboaj) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx 0.3$ and where $x\approx 4.7$.

We already know $x\approx 0.3$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 4.7$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 4.7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$\sqrt{3x+3}=e^x$

One of the solutions is $x\approx -1$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=\sqrt{3x+3}$ and $g(x)=e^x$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/tlspackeko) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -1$ and where $x\approx 0.9$.

We already know $x\approx -1$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 0.9$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 0.9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$4x^2-16x+13=3\log_2(x)$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 1.1$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=4x^2-16x+13$ and $g(x)=3\log_2(x)$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/u1hlguetg0) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx 1.1$ and where $x\approx 3.4$.

We already know $x\approx 1.1$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 3.4$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 3.4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$x^3+3x^2-x=|x-1|$

Two of the solutions are $x\approx -0.7$ and $x\approx 0.5$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=x^3+3x^2-x$ and $g(x)=|x-1|$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/dp3jliopsy) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -2.9$, and where $x\approx -0.7$, and where $x\approx 0.5$.

We already know $x\approx -0.7$ and $x\approx 0.5$ are solutions, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx -2.9$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx -2.9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$|x-4|=x^2-6x+9$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 1.4$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=|x-4|$ and $g(x)=x^2-6x+9$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/hgnnvv3kfh) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx 1.4$ and where $x\approx 3.6$.

We already know $x\approx 1.4$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 3.6$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 3.6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$\sqrt{2x+8}=5\log_3(x)$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 352$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=\sqrt{2x+8}$ and $g(x)=5\log_3(x)$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/fabqldfy1p) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 2]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx 2.2$ and where $x\approx 352$.

We already know $x\approx 352$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 2.2$. Here is the same graph with a smaller range so we can zoom in on that solution:

[[☃ image 1]]
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 2.2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$\sqrt[3]{x+1}=2x+2$

Two of the solutions are $x\approx -0.6$ and $x=-1$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=\sqrt[3]{x+1}$ and $g(x)=2x+2$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/prme3uut8p) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -1.4$, and where $x=-1$, and where $x\approx -0.6$.

We already know $x\approx -0.6$ and $x=-1$ are solutions, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx -1.4$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx -1.4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$x^3-4x=2^x$

Three of the solutions are $x\approx -2.0$, $x\approx-0.2$, and $x\approx 9.8$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=x^3-4x$ and $g(x)=2^x$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/61wgyuqft7) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -2.0$, $x\approx-0.2$, and $x\approx 2.5$ (they also intersect when $x\approx 9.8$ but that's out of the range of this graph).

We already know $x\approx -2.0$ and $x\approx-0.2$ are solutions, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 2.5$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 2.5$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$x^2-4=\sqrt[3]{2x}$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 2.4$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=x^2-4$ and $g(x)=\sqrt[3]{2x}$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/iixwxd2yb9) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -1.6$ and where $x\approx 2.4$.

We already know $x\approx 2.4$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx -1.6$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx -1.6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$x+2=\sqrt{5x+5}$

One of the solutions is $x\approx -0.6$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=x+2$ and $g(x)=\sqrt{5x+5}$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/hmjuqfxfni) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx -0.6$ and where $x\approx 1.6$.

We already know $x\approx -0.6$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 1.6$.
In conclusion, the other solution is $x\approx 1.6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want to solve the following equation.

$3\log_2(x)=x^3-4x^2+4x$

One of the solutions is $x\approx 3.3$.

**Find the other solution.**  
*Hint: Use a graphing calculator.*  
*Round your answer to the nearest tenth.*

$x\approx $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ intersect are the solutions of the equation $f(x)=g(x)$.

In our case, $f(x)=3\log_2(x)$ and $g(x)=x^3-4x^2+4x$. What we need is to graph them, and then look for intersection points.

If you have a graphing calculator, now would be a good time to use it! If not, there are graphing calculators available online, for example [Desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator).

Here are the graphs. You can also [click this link](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/vthnlrmzbx) to see the graphs on Desmos.

[[☃ image 1]]

The graphs intersect at the points where $x\approx 1.2$ and where $x\approx 3.3$.

We already know $x\approx 3.3$ is a solution, so the other solution we are looking for is $x\approx 1.2$.
In conclusion, the solution is $x\approx 1.2$.
